title: Aalius quadrangularis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Aalius quadrangularis." It's possible that it's a misspelling or a non-existent plant.

However, I can suggest a similar plant, "Aleurites moluccanus" or "Tung oil tree" which is a plant in the Euphorbiaceae family. 

Aleurites moluccanus is a plant with medicinal properties. 

Here's a paragraph about Aleurites moluccanus:

Aleurites moluccanus, also known as the Tung oil tree, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and it has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Aleurites moluccanus include aleuritic acid, tung oil, and other triterpenoid compounds. Aleurites moluccanus is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine, and its oil is often used in herbal preparations, such as topical creams and ointments, to treat skin conditions like eczema and wounds.
